channel.title: AI Agents by Industry on The Multi-Agent Future
channel.description: Recent content in AI Agents by Industry on The Multi-Agent Future

item.title: How Multi-Agent AI Is Replacing $200/Hour Legal Associates in Document Review
item.description:
The $10 Billion Document Review Problem

When a company gets sued — or sues someone — the first phase is discovery. Both sides must produce relevant documents. In a typical corporate litigation case, this means reviewing 500,000 to 5 million documents to determine which are relevant, privileged, or responsive.

At major law firms, this work is done by junior associates and contract attorneys billing $150-400/hour. According to the RAND Corporation’s analysis of litigation costs (RAND, 2012), document review accounts for 60-80% of total discovery costs in large cases.
